audit_status.source_base: Greek Acts text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Malta, viper, Publius healings, voyage to Rome, Roman Judean leaders, Isaiah hearing text, final bold unhindered proclamation.

verses.restored: When we had been brought safely through, then we learned that the island was called Malta.
The islanders showed us no ordinary kindness; kindling a fire, they welcomed us all because of the rain that had set in and because of the cold.
Paul gathered a bundle of sticks and placed it on the fire; a viper came out from the heat and fastened on his hand.
When the islanders saw the creature hanging from his hand, they were saying to one another, Surely this human is a murderer, whom, though saved/healed from the sea, Justice has not allowed to live.
But he shook off the creature into the fire and suffered no harm.
They were expecting him to swell up or suddenly fall down dead. But after waiting a long time and seeing nothing out of place happening to him, changing their minds, they said he was a god.
Now in the places around that site were lands belonging to the first man of the island, named Publius, who received us and hosted us kindly three days.
It happened that the father of Publius was lying down, afflicted with fevers and dysentery. Paul went in to him, prayed, laid hands on him, and healed him.
When this happened, the rest of those on the island who had weaknesses also came and were being healed.
They honored us with many honors, and when we were putting out to sea, they put on board the things we needed.
After three months we put out to sea in an Alexandrian ship that had wintered on the island, whose sign was the Twin Brothers.
Putting in at Syracuse, we remained three days.
From there, making a circuit, we arrived at Rhegium; after one day a south wind came up, and on the second day we came to Puteoli.
There, finding siblings, we were invited to remain with them seven days. And so we came to Rome.
From there the siblings, hearing the things concerning us, came to meet us as far as the Forum of Appius and Three Taverns. Seeing them, Paul gave thanks to the Divine and took courage.
When we entered Rome, Paul was permitted to remain by himself with the soldier guarding him.

After three days he called together those who were first among the Judeans. When they had gathered, he said to them, Men, siblings, though I had done nothing against the people or ancestral customs, I was handed over as prisoner from Jerusalem into the hands of the Romans.
After examining me, they wished to release me because there was no cause of death in me.
But when the Judeans spoke against it, I was compelled to appeal to Caesar, not as though having anything to accuse my nation of.
Because of this cause therefore I called you to see and speak with you, for because of the hope of Israel I am wearing this chain.
They said to him, We have neither received letters about you from Judea, nor has any of the siblings arriving reported or spoken anything harmful concerning you.
But we consider it worthy to hear from you what you think, for concerning this sect, it is known to us that everywhere it is spoken against.

Arranging a day with him, many came to him at the lodging, to whom he explained, bearing witness to the reign of the Divine and persuading them concerning Jesus from both the Law of Moses and the Prophets, from morning until evening.
Some were persuaded by the things spoken, but others were not trusting.
Being discordant with one another, they departed after Paul said one word: Beautifully/well did the holy Spirit speak through Isaiah the prophet to your fathers,
saying, Go to this people and say, Hearing you will hear and certainly not understand, and seeing you will see and certainly not perceive.

For the heart of this people has grown thick, and with the ears they heard heavily, and their eyes they closed, lest they should see with the eyes and hear with the ears and understand with the heart and turn, and I would heal them.
Therefore let it be known to you that this salvation/healing of the Divine has been sent to the nations; they also will hear.
[Some later manuscripts add that after he said these things, the Judeans departed, having much dispute among themselves.]
He remained two whole years in his own rented lodging and welcomed all those coming to him,
proclaiming the reign of the Divine and teaching the things concerning the Lord Jesus Messiah with all boldness, unhindered.
